title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
[MUSIC PLAYING] IDA ASSAYESH: Hi, I'm
Ida, the Product Lead for the Gamer Profile on Play. HEMANT PAWAR: And I'm
Hemant Pawar, a Product Lead for Play Game Services. Play Game Services or PGS
is a foundational piece of technology with features
designed to help enhance the engagement, social
aspects, and overall success of your games across Android
and Windows platform. In today's talk, we will
discuss how Play Game Services can level up your game on Play. We will also be showcasing
two exciting features, game achievements
and gen AI avatars. You will also learn
about the migration path to PGS V2 and ways to
accelerate business benefits by preparing ahead for PGS
V1 sunset timeline of 2028. Let's get started. Over the past few years,
Play Game Services has grown significantly
with users and developers. The Gamer Profile,
where PGS integration is materialized for users,
has more than 570 million monthly active users. Six of the top 10 games on
Android are integrated with PGS. Not just top studios,
but indie games also drive engagement
and monetization using one or more PGS features. Many developers
already use PGS today to enable gameplay
continuity across devices. This is specifically
important, as in today's world, users frequently switch back
and forth from one device to another. We also know that social
elements, such as competition and community, are key
drivers for gamer motivation. Developers use
leaderboards from PGS to appeal to gamer motivation
and drive additional user engagement. Additionally, the
PGS Events API allows you to configure critical events
for your game and store gameplay data around these
events in Google servers for game analytics. This year, we are doubling
down our investment in Gamer Profile and PGS, making
PGS an even more powerful tool for developers. PGS and the Gamer Profile
are the means for you and your gamers to access
all the new features on Play. PGS connects your
game with Play. PGS is how you
communicate with Play and how we communicate with you.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
PGS and the Gamer Profile
are the means for you and your gamers to access
all the new features on Play. PGS connects your
game with Play. PGS is how you
communicate with Play and how we communicate with you. Ida will now talk
about some key features coming to the Gamer Profile. IDA ASSAYESH: Now let's chat
about some exciting features Play has in store that
take both the user and developer experience
to the next level. The Gamer Profile delivers
a personalized connection to games, enabling
players to stay engaged, customize their experience, and
unlock a suite of features that consistently enhance gameplay. Players and developers alike
gain significant benefits from the gamer profile
and PGS integrations. Today, I'm excited to talk
about two key features and how they can enhance your
game's success with Play, achievements and gen AI avatars. Let's start with achievements. These are developer-provided,
title-specific achievements that players can earn
throughout their gaming journey, such as gathering a
certain number of items or reaching new levels. Games that have
incorporated achievements create a compelling
gaming experience that motivates players
to download the game and then continue
to play the game. Achievements are
also a powerful tool for developers who want to
grow their games with Play. Not only are games with
achievements oftentimes more enticing for players,
but these games also have increased
visibility across Play. Achievements are highlighted
in the Details page of titles, allowing users to continuously
track their progress. Players can also compare their
achievements with one another through the game
or profile, keeping competition high and
achievements top of mind. When a game has
achievements, it can also be featured as an
achievement based quest. These play rewarded mechanics
can drive significant results, like increased downloads
and re-engagement from existing players. In fact, these campaigns target
both new and existing users. For individual titles, we have
seen quests generate upwards
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
These play rewarded mechanics
can drive significant results, like increased downloads
and re-engagement from existing players. In fact, these campaigns target
both new and existing users. For individual titles, we have
seen quests generate upwards of 100,000 new installs
and re-engagements. All of this is possible
by adding achievements into your game. Now that we've established
how vital achievements are to your game's
success, let's explore what the best, most effective
achievement configurations look like. Here are some actionable tips
for creating great achievements. And remember, when achievements
are fun and attainable, they can drive
engagement and encourage users to try new things, which
is proven to increase retention. We've seen that gamers with the
best success do the following. Reward the gamer throughout
their lifetime with the game. This can mean many different
things for your specific game, but typically, that looks
more than 20 achievements, with the ideal state
being between 50 and 100. Consider having the player
unlock their first five achievements within the
first two hours of gameplay. A frequent cadence of
unlocking achievements can keep users motivated
throughout the lifespan of the game. Match game completion
with your achievements, or ensure that users are earning
achievements at a rate that is encouraging to them. All achievements must have an
asset associated with them. At a minimum, you can
provide a single image across all achievements. However, using different
images for each achievement is the best way to make
them visually appealing and attract user attention
across the store. It is also important
to have diversity in the types of achievements
your game offers. Consider creating achievements
around game progression, leveling up characters or bases,
Easter eggs, incremental events, and even rewarding
trying and failing. Achievements are
earned in three states, earned, unearned,
and in progress. Don't forget to add progression
to your achievements.
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
leveling up characters or bases,
Easter eggs, incremental events, and even rewarding
trying and failing. Achievements are
earned in three states, earned, unearned,
and in progress. Don't forget to add progression
to your achievements. Players love to see
how they are tracking towards earning an achievement. It is also a great way to
keep momentum and gameplay up. Kingdom Rush 5 is
a great example of what high quality
achievements look like. Different image assets,
hidden surprises per level, players
getting rewarded in-game with gems when they
earn an achievement, and game completion is when
all achievements are earned, which is a very
satisfying experience. Now let's move on
to another feature. Gen AI avatars. Gen AI avatars are a new and
highly anticipated feature-- so exciting that it
recently leaked-- that is coming soon to
Play and the Gamer Profile. These AI powered avatars
represent a significant upgrade from the profile's
current static options. This enhancement
is a powerful tool for building personalization and
fostering loyalty among users, allowing them to create unique
digital representations which can easily be leveraged
within your games. Play is dedicated to upleveling
and enriching the overall player experience, providing more
engaging and expressive ways for users to connect and
showcase their gaming worlds. Users are able to generate AI
powered avatars that are fun, diverse, and represent
their gaming selves, allowing them to put their
personal stamp on an avatar that is all their own. We guide users through
the AI generation process, featuring a number of exciting
characters, backgrounds, and art styles to choose from that will
produce high quality results. With an avatar that
is uniquely theirs, players are more invested
in their gaming experience. But these avatars won't just
live in the Gamer Profile on Play. With an easy PGS
integration, developers can opt to leverage a player's
avatar within their games. This way, your
users can continue
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
players are more invested
in their gaming experience. But these avatars won't just
live in the Gamer Profile on Play. With an easy PGS
integration, developers can opt to leverage a player's
avatar within their games. This way, your
users can continue to express themselves with their
AI avatar across all your games. Avatars are just one of many
exciting and personalized updates to the gamer
profile that are to come. Stay tuned to see what PGS and
their profile will unlock next. Now I'll hand it
over to Hemant, who will also speak about how to
accelerate value with PGS. HEMANT PAWAR: This is where
it all comes together. PGS enables all
of these features for you and your gamers. With achievements
feature, you send us achievements config via
Play Console and unlock trigger via API call, and you get
enriched presence of game on Play, improved
discoverability via search on Play Store. With quest feature, you send
us achievements and campaign details, and we create
and manage quests for you. Your users are rewarded
with Play Points, and you get incremental installs
and monetization for your game. With recall feature, you
generate and send us tokens, and we store these
tokens with Play, linking your users
to PGS accounts. This gets you
ability to recognize your users across games,
devices, and reinstalls. With this, you can also easily
take your games to Windows platform and expand on PC. As you may have noted,
all of the exciting features we discussed
require PGS integration. Great news is that
it doesn't have to be all or nothing
with PGS integration. We understand that game
needs vary for each of you as a game developer. Hence we have built PGS
as a modular system. Let's look at different paths
to get value quicker from PGS. But before going deeper
into integration options, we want to share an important
update with you about PGS. PGS currently has two SDK and
API sets, PGS V1 and PGS V2. We are unifying the PGS solution
and PGS V1 is being deprecated. We recently announced the
sunset, but as a quick reminder,
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
we want to share an important
update with you about PGS. PGS currently has two SDK and
API sets, PGS V1 and PGS V2. We are unifying the PGS solution
and PGS V1 is being deprecated. We recently announced the
sunset, but as a quick reminder, let's look at the key timelines. Since March 2025, developers
cannot publish titles with new PGS V1 integrations. If you are planning a new PGS
integration, you must use V2. PGS V1 APIs will be
removed effective May 2026 and completely shut
down in Q3 2028. Existing integrations will
continue to work till Q3 2028, but we recommend you to
migrate before the end of 2025 to be ready to take advantage
of all the new features that we talked about. For resources on
migration, please refer to the game dev center. We recommend integrating the
base SDK and achievements feature to begin with. This will unlock the benefits
of achievements and quests for your users and you. Most developers
take about one week to integrate the base
SDK and about two weeks to integrate achievements. We recognize the
efforts involved in creating the recommended
number of achievements for your game, and hence, we
have made it super easy for you. Now you can create
achievements in bulk in Play Console using CSV
files and import utility. Read more about the new
features at Gamedev Center and try it out for your game. You can fast follow achievements
with the integration of recall feature to link your
identity systems with PGS and support continuity
across devices. We estimate about one week to
integrate the recall feature. In about four weeks,
your game is already well-positioned to deliver
amazing experiences to your users. After this minimum
suggested integration, you can opt for
integrating other features, such as leaderboards and events
based on your game needs. Learn more about these
features at Gamedev Center. Back to you, Ida. IDA ASSAYESH: Thank
you for tuning in to hear the latest
about the Gamer Profile and Play Game Services. We're excited to
partner with you
speaker: unknown

title: Google I/O 2025 – v-6b_wWF96U
text:
Learn more about these
features at Gamedev Center. Back to you, Ida. IDA ASSAYESH: Thank
you for tuning in to hear the latest
about the Gamer Profile and Play Game Services. We're excited to
partner with you to land these new
experiences for your users, and make sure to check
out the documentation to learn more about
migration to PGS V2, and keep your eye out for all of
the new, exciting features that are coming soon in
2025 and beyond. [MUSIC PLAYING]
